summary:
GlanzMann thrombasthenia is caused by genetic mutations in platelet integrin subunits (αIIbβ3), leading to a congenital defect in platelet function essential for aggregation. It is not characterized by a deficiency of plasma clotting factors but rather an inherent malfunction of platelets during primary hemostasis.  

Answer: \boxed{A} Based only on the above text, select one answer among these options:

(A). Congenital defect of platelets  
(B). Congenital defect of RBCs  
(C). Defect of Neutrophils  
(D). Clotting factor deficiency

Only output the letter of your choice. Do NOT add explanations.

A
